column48: Andy is responsible for the full UK and Ireland P&L at LeoVegas, with accountability for the commercial performance of the local brands, leveraging central group capabilities across gaming, sportsbook, payments, and risk while ensuring local market priorities are delivered.
column50: Affiliate & Acquisition: LeoVegas is looking at investing in affiliate-led acquisition and broader customer growth initiatives, with a strong emphasis on acquiring high-value players as efficiently as possible. With the brand still firmly in growth mode in core markets, the focus is on driving scale while keeping acquisition costs tightly controlled, ensuring that marketing spend translates into sustainable, profitable revenue rather than short-term volume.
column51: KYC & IDV: LeoVegas aims to invest in KYC and identity verification to improve the casino onboarding journey while meeting increasingly complex regulatory requirements. The emphasis is on reducing friction for legitimate players, speeding up time to first deposit, and managing cost and risk more effectively, ensuring compliance does not come at the expense of player experience or conversion.
column52: AI & Predictive Analytics / Personalization: LeoVegas is looking into data, AI, and predictive analytics to drive more personalised casino experiences and improve retention. By better understanding player behaviour and preferences, the business aims to tailor gameplay, offers, and journeys more effectively, using insight-led personalisation to support loyalty and maximise lifetime value in an increasingly competitive casino landscape.

column48: Razvan is responsible for the full day-to-day operations and P&L of Gets Bet’s online brands. His remit spans support, risk, fraud, casino operations, retention, payments and overall performance across their Romanian market portfolio.
column50: Casino Platform: Gets Bet is looking at investing in game platform providers to support both content scalability and future market expansion. As a multi-brand operator currently active in Romania with ambitions to expand regionally, ensuring a flexible and robust platform architecture is essential. Upgrading or enhancing platform capabilities would enable smoother integrations, improved performance management and faster deployment of new content verticals.
column51: AI & Predictive Analytics/ Personalization: Gets Bet is looking at investing in AI and predictive analytics to enhance decision-making across casino operations. Razvan expressed strong interest in expanding AI usage to its “full capacity”, leveraging their substantial data volumes to drive smarter segmentation, faster analysis and improved commercial insight. The objective is to use real-time data processing and predictive modelling to optimise performance across retention, risk and player engagement.
column52: Market Research: Gets Bet is looking at investing in market research and player insights to better understand behavioural trends and evolving player expectations. Razvan confirmed that analytics already plays a significant role in day-to-day operations, and that deeper insight generation remains a strategic focus area.

column48: Supporting operations and commercial teams; remit includes customer service, VIP Management, Risk, fraud and payments, BI and Data, commercial and product, marketing.
column50:
Gamification, Loyalty & Rewards Optimisation:
PowerPlay is focused on enhancing its gamification and loyalty strategy, with the aim of improving bonusing mechanics across the entire customer base rather than limiting offers to VIP segments. A key objective is to drive stronger retention while ensuring promotions remain cost-effective and revenue-generating. The current model is viewed as suboptimal, prompting a search for more advanced systems or processes. There is also an interest in identifying solutions that can maximise engagement and value across venues.
column51:
Omnichannel Expansion & New Product Offerings:
PowerPlay is exploring opportunities to expand its online and omnichannel presence, including the potential introduction of bingo or similar products. This initiative is driven by a desire to generate additional revenue streams and broaden audience reach. While there have been ongoing efforts to better connect casino and sportsbook customers, the focus is now shifting towards introducing complementary offerings to strengthen the overall proposition. The goal is to create a more seamless and diversified customer experience.
column52:
AI-Driven Personalisation & Engagement:
PowerPlay is actively investigating the use of AI to enhance personalisation, particularly in relation to gamification and bonusing strategies. There is a strong interest in leveraging AI-driven insights to better understand customer behaviour and optimise interactions. As part of this, the business is considering upgrading its existing software stack to support more advanced capabilities. This includes the potential implementation of an AI-powered chatbot to improve customer engagement and service delivery.

column48: Tuf is responsible for leading AI-enabled customer safety products across Sky Bet, Paddy Power, Betfair, and PokerStars. The role oversees all safer gambling initiatives, including identifying at-risk customers, designing effective intervention strategies to prevent harm, and reducing unnecessary friction within the customer journey. His remit also includes driving a product-led transformation across Flutter, embedding scalable ways of working and advancing responsible gaming through data and technology innovation.
column50:
AI & Predictive Analytics (Customer Personalisation):
Flutter is developing an in-house AI and predictive analytics capability focused on personalising the customer experience across the full product journey. The objective is to enhance internal products through external tools and insights that strengthen data-driven personalisation, automation, and behavioural engagement strategies. They are particularly interested in solution providers adapting front-end experiences through behavioural data, similar to e-commerce personalisation models, including real-time prompts and dynamic UX customisation. The scope spans homepage experiences, loyalty programmes, sportsbook, and gaming environments. This initiative also closely aligns with safer gambling objectives and long-term customer loyalty development.
column51:
Safer Gambling & Player Protection:
Safer gambling and player protection represent a key strategic focus, with Flutter actively exploring innovative and white-label solutions in this space. The first priority is improving the identification of potentially harmful player behaviour and strengthening intervention mechanisms to better protect customers. In parallel, the business is assessing where existing controls may unintentionally create friction for low-risk players, with the aim of refining models to avoid unnecessary restrictions and unlock incremental revenue. A core challenge is that risky behaviour exists within a grey area rather than a binary classification, requiring more advanced behavioural analysis and detection tools. The organisation is therefore interested in technologies that can more accurately identify risk signals while balancing protection and customer experience
column52:
Omnichannel Loyalty, Retention & Reactivation:
Flutter is exploring omnichannel loyalty strategies to improve retention and increase customer lifetime value across multiple gambling products. With relatively low switching barriers in the industry, the goal is to identify solutions that strengthen customer defensibility through targeted engagement and behavioural incentives. Particular focus is placed on encouraging multi-product adoption and repeat engagement while maintaining responsible gambling standards. The organisation is interested in understanding how different tools measure loyalty effectiveness and influence player behaviour across channels. Providers offering solutions that balance commercial growth with responsible gaming outcomes are of strong interest.

column48: Sam is currently shaping FDJ United's growth priorities and corporate strategy. Sam works within the Kindred business unit, focusing on online betting and gaming.
column50: Localisation & Market Expansion: FDJ is focused on improving localisation across key growth markets, particularly in Eastern Europe (e.g. Romania), where their current presence is limited. They recognise that marketing strategies must be tailored by region, including differences in frequency, touchpoints, and reward structures, as current efforts lack sufficient localisation. There is also interest in exploring region-specific products such as sports lotteries, with internal teams in Sweden already assessing opportunities. Previous expansion attempts, such as in the US, highlighted shortcomings in localisation, reinforcing its importance for future growth in regions like Asia and LATAM.
column51: AML & Customer Journey Optimisation: As a highly regulated organisation, AML compliance remains a critical priority, alongside maintaining safer gambling standards. However, they are facing challenges in balancing these requirements with customer experience, as increased friction in onboarding and verification journeys is negatively impacting conversion rates. FDJ is actively seeking more seamless, frictionless solutions that can streamline these processes while maintaining compliance. Building greater trust within the journey is also key, particularly as players are hesitant to share personal data with gaming authorities, with potential interest in solutions such as bank-linked verification to improve confidence and reduce drop-off.
column52: CX/UX & Product Competitiveness: FDJ has identified a gap in CX compared to illegal operators, which is impacting their ability to remain competitive. Sam is particularly interested in evolving their casino offering, including the types of games available and how they are presented to users. He admires the approach of Stake.com in effectively tailoring the experience to customer preferences. While they have begun introducing similar game formats, traction has been limited so far, prompting questions around timing, execution, and the broader direction of the future casino experience.

column50: OMNICHANNEL: FDJ is currently placing a strong focus on enhancing the overall player experience across its gaming portfolio. The company is therefore looking to invest in omnichannel capabilities that can create a more seamless and connected cross-vertical player journey.
column51: GAME CONTENT PROVIDERS: FDJ are exploring partnerships with game content providers that can offer differentiated products and experiences beyond what is already widely available in the market. There is a particular interest in niche-focused content and innovative concepts that can help the brand stand out competitively. FDJ is also seeking solutions that can be tailored to different regional markets and player segments. In addition, there is interest in next-generation gaming technology that can support future growth and engagement strategies.
column52: SOCIAL CASINO: The company is currently not heavily active within the social casino space, although it does operate a basic free-to-play offering. The team is keen to stay updated on developments within the sector and explore more advanced social casino solutions. Their primary focus is on engagement-driven products that can also serve as effective acquisition and marketing tools. They are interested in platforms and features that can increase player interaction, retention, and brand reach through social and free-to-play mechanics.
